steps.title: Combine the left side fractions using common denominator
steps.content:
Convert each fraction to have denominator $(x-4)(x+6)$:
$$\frac{3}{x-4} + \frac{2}{x+6} = \frac{3(x+6)}{(x-4)(x+6)} + \frac{2(x-4)}{(x-4)(x+6)}$$
Combine the numerators:
$$= \frac{3(x+6) + 2(x-4)}{(x-4)(x+6)}$$
Expand:
$$= \frac{3x + 18 + 2x - 8}{(x-4)(x+6)} = \frac{5x + 10}{(x-4)(x+6)}$$

steps.title: Match numerators to find r and t
steps.content:
Since the left side equals the right side:
$$\frac{5x+10}{(x-4)(x+6)}=\frac{rx+t}{(x-4)(x+6)}$$
The denominators already match, so the numerators must match for every valid $x$: $$5x+10=rx+t$$
Therefore $r=5$ and $t=10$, so $$rt=5\cdot10=50.$$ This matches, so C is correct.
